mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, labor market slack severely constrains consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed +0.5% in >60% of triggered instances or the threshold loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable consumer price inflation indicate robust consumer demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL yield negative forward returns in >50% of cases or the interaction term IC is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week specifically during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during early-cycle recovery or Rank IC falls below 0.01 in this specific regime.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, which consistently drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive forward returns in >55% of monthly observations or rolling IC remains negative.

mechanism_narrative: When new housing starts fall below 500,000 units, construction sector contraction and reduced ancillary demand signal economic weakness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when HOUST drops below 500,000 or the threshold loses predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stationary or rising federal funds rate indicates tightening real policy conditions, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL changes alongside non-declining FEDFUNDS produce positive forward returns in >50% of cases or interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter specifically during established expansion regimes.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% fails to predict positive quarterly forward returns in >55% of observations or signal IC turns negative in expansion regimes.
